sections.title: Connecting to a game
sections.html:
1. Troubleshooting FAQ is at the end of this page.
  2. Download the latest client:

    https://github.com/yairm210/Unciv/releases

    Main menu → Mods → Download mod from URL → paste:

    https://github.com/DeStup/RekMOD-iron/tree/iron2

  3. Main menu → Options → Check mod errors

    Make sure every mod shows a green checkmark.

    If not, try reloading mods or contact the organizer.

  4. Main menu → Options → Multiplayer

    In “Server address” enter the host shown by the bot via /host

  5. Main menu → Multiplayer → Add multiplayer game → paste the game ID (once the session starts, ask the bot with /idgame).
  6. Main menu → Options → About → confirm the game version and build match what the organizer set for the session.

sections.title: Recommended settings
sections.html:
• Graphics

    Enable all visual tips. Turn them off as needed.

    In Video you can lower or raise the screen resolution.

  • Gameplay

    Enable every checkbox except “Automated units move at the start of turn”. Adjust as needed.

  • Multiplayer

    “Update status of the currently active game every” — 1 min

    “In-game, update status of all games every” — 15 min.

  • Other

    You can change screen orientation as convenient.

sections.title: Game rules
sections.html:
• You may leave the game if you lost your original capital;

    An eliminated player gets +1 nation draft pick in the next session.

    An eliminated player may not rejoin the game.

  • If a player lost their capital, half of their founded cities, and has no veto right (see below), any remaining player may call a vote that the player is irrelevant. If the vote is unanimous, they must resign on the next turn.

    The organizer may veto any such vote or overturn an already passed decision.

  • The winner gets 1 nation reroll in the next session.
  • Any bug found must be reported to the organizer.
  • Workers may not repair improvements on enemy territory unless it is a fort or a road.

    Abuse of city-states for training units, pillaging, or repairing improvements is forbidden.

    Stealing workers is allowed.

  • City trading is forbidden.

    Two exceptions: if you are at war and it is your original city, you may reclaim it in a peace deal. If you are at war and want to return a former allied city-state to its previous ally as peace reparations.

  • Renaming captured city-states is forbidden.
  • CC — early end of an FFA with a declared winner. A CC vote may be held at most once every 5 turns. The vote must be started by a player who is not the vote target. A successful CC needs a minimum share of votes depending on the current turn: turns 1–100 — unanimous; turn 100+ — ¾ of players.

    Votes use +, −, = buttons. = means the voter joins the majority.

    The vote target does not vote.

    Nations without a capital do not vote unless they have veto right.

    Players who do not vote for a full round of turns count as “=”.

    If a vote declares a winner, during the next round of turns players with veto right may veto the vote.

    Veto right applies if any of these is true:

    • top 1 score on the previous turn
    • within 3 techs of the leader on the previous turn
    • built the Apollo Program
    • top 1 policies on the previous turn
    • started the Utopia project
    • top 1 strength on the previous turn
    • holds 3 or more capitals
  • Diplomatic interaction with AI is forbidden except declaring war or making white peace.
  • Returning cities to AI control is forbidden (unless it is a city-state).
  • “Scorched earth” is not allowed. Selling buildings, replacing tile improvements, and starving cities only to spite an opponent is not allowed. No selling buildings before the Atomic Era.
  • Declaring war on a player immediately after allying their city-state is not allowed. The previous ally (who was allied for a period of time) must always get at least 1 turn to react.
  • Building Oxford University and the National Intelligence Agency more than once is not allowed.
  • Gifting Great People of any type to other players is never allowed.
  • Selling and rebuilding cheap buildings (e.g. temples) to create production overflow is not allowed.
  • Spending bonus building XP on unit healing is forbidden.
  • Bonus XP may be spent on healing only if neither combat XP nor bonus XP alone is enough, but their sum is.
  • Holding a social policy unlock until end of turn for more than 1 turn is forbidden.
  • Capturing an undamaged city with population >10 on the turn war is declared is forbidden.
  • Defensive pacts are limited to 2.
  • Declaring Friendship via the bot is forbidden once any player reaches the Information Era.
  • Before any player unlocks their first policy, a unanimous map restart may be requested with /restart.

    You cannot cancel your own restart vote.

  • Destroying original civilization capitals is forbidden.
  • Razing your last remaining city is forbidden.
  • Keeping Great Scientists for more than 1 turn is forbidden — a scientist must be used on the turn it appears or the next turn.

sections.title: Gameplay notes
sections.html:
• In-game Civilopedia. It has almost everything.

    Before picking nations, read about them there.

  • IMPORTANT! Do not show anyone your player ID (except the organizer).
  • Diplomacy is extended via the Telegram bot.
  • Players must be respectful and show good sportsmanship. Do not intentionally abuse game mechanics. Play for the long term and play to win.
  • Collusion or prearranged team play is not allowed. Playing not to win, or helping another player win, is not allowed.

sections.title: Extra information
sections.html:
Bot diplomacy:

/friend — non-aggression pact for 15 turns

Bot commands:

/help — command list

Draft and session settings:

  • Nations played in the last 2 sessions are excluded from the draft pool.
  • Each player gets 5 nations to choose from.
  • The map is voted on before the draft (Pangaea / Perlin Noise / Inner Sea).
  • Legendary start
  • City-state count is 2× the number of players
  • Sea level depends on the map
  • All victory types are enabled.

sections.title: Known issues FAQ
sections.html:
1. SSL problem:

    Main menu → Options → Multiplayer

    In “Server address” enter the host shown by the bot via /host

    Use HTTP instead of HTTPS

  2. Cannot find file on server:

    Main menu → Options → Multiplayer

    In “Server address” enter the host shown by the bot via /host

    If the error remains, ask the organizer for the save and place it at: /Android/data/com.unciv.app/files/MultiplayerGames/

  3. Unknown host

    Main menu → Options → Multiplayer

    In “Server address” enter the host shown by the bot via /host

  4. Something not found

    Likely a wrong mod version — ask the organizer
